message:
mcp-server v0.9: rulake_list_collections tool — closes ADR-006 server-gap #1

Adds a Read-tier MCP tool that enumerates the collections a registered
backend exposes. The Console's Browse screen has fixture data today;
this is the wire it'll consume in iteration 14 to flip Browse from
fixture to live (deep-review §9, server-side gap #1).

src/lake.rs (rulake crate)
  - New `pub fn list_collections(&self, id: &str)` — looks up the
    backend in the read-locked registry and delegates to its
    `BackendAdapter::list_collections()`. Returns
    RuLakeError::UnknownBackend if the id isn't registered.

mcp-server/src/server.rs
  - New `#[tool] rulake_list_collections(backend: String)`. Read-tier:
    `require_cap(Capability::Read)` gates it. Submitted to the bounded
    rayon worker pool (same hygiene as rulake_query — backend can
    legitimately do disk I/O). Maps RuLakeError::UnknownBackend to
    `McpError::invalid_params("UNKNOWN_BACKEND: <id>")` so callers see
    a 400-shape error not a 500.
  - New types: `ListCollectionsArgs { backend }`, `CollectionEntry { id }`,
    `ListCollectionsResponse { backend, collections }`. CollectionEntry
    is one-field for now (CollectionId is currently a String alias);
    when src/backend.rs grows dim/row_count/witness fields, the
    response type extends without a wire break.
  - tool_capability table (line 458) updated: rulake_list_collections
    joins rulake_query and rulake_list_backends in the Read tier. The
    tools/list visibility filter (v0.4) automatically drops it from
    the listing when the request's CapabilitySet doesn't include Read.

mcp-server/tests/smoke.rs
  - tools_list_filtered_by_capability_set:
      read-only: now expects 3 tools (was 2) — rulake_list_backends,
        rulake_list_collections, rulake_query
      admin:     now expects 8 tools (was 7) — adds rulake_list_collections

  All preserved test counts:
    cargo test --release      → 37 unit + 8 e2e + 20 smoke + 1 ignored
    ./scripts/smoke.sh --live → all 6 audit codes green, 0 console errors

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
